SEARCH
Package neqsim.thermo.component.attractiveeosterm

Class AttractiveTermMatCop5PRUMR

java.lang.Object
neqsim.thermo.component.attractiveeosterm.AttractiveTermBaseClass
neqsim.thermo.component.attractiveeosterm.AttractiveTermPr
neqsim.thermo.component.attractiveeosterm.AttractiveTermMatCop5PRUMR
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Cloneable, AttractiveTermInterface
public class AttractiveTermMatCop5PRUMR extends AttractiveTermPr

AttractiveTermMatCop5PRUMR class.

Five-parameter Mathias-Copeman alpha function for the Peng-Robinson based UMR-CPA equation of state. The alpha function reads

alpha(T) = [1 + c1 (1 - sqrt(Tr)) + c2 (1 - sqrt(Tr))^2 + c3 (1 - sqrt(Tr))^3 + c4 (1 - sqrt(Tr))^4 + c5 (1 - sqrt(Tr))^5]^2

The five-parameter form is used by Tasios et al. (Fluid Phase Equilibria, 2025, doi:10.1016/j.fluid.2024.114241) for the non-self-associating compounds in the UMR-CPA model. The five coefficients are dimensionless and are stored in the UMRCPA_MC1..UMRCPA_MC5 columns of COMP.csv.

Version:
$Id: $Id
Author:
esol

  • Field Details

    • serialVersionUID

      private static final long serialVersionUID
      Serialization version UID.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • mcParams

      private double[] mcParams
      Five Mathias-Copeman coefficients c1..c5.

    • useStandardAlphaForSupercritical

      private boolean useStandardAlphaForSupercritical
      Use the standard PR alpha for supercritical temperatures. The UMR-PRU/UMR-CPA family extrapolates the Mathias-Copeman polynomial above the critical temperature (consistent with AtractiveTermMatCopPRUMRNew), so this defaults to false.

  • Constructor Details

    • AttractiveTermMatCop5PRUMR

      public AttractiveTermMatCop5PRUMR(ComponentEosInterface component)

      Constructor for AttractiveTermMatCop5PRUMR.

      Parameters:

      public AttractiveTermMatCop5PRUMR(ComponentEosInterface component, double[] params)

      Constructor for AttractiveTermMatCop5PRUMR.

      Parameters:
      component - a ComponentEosInterface object
      params - an array of type double with up to five Mathias-Copeman coefficients

  • Method Details

    • clone

      public AttractiveTermMatCop5PRUMR clone()

      a AttractiveTermInterface object

    • useStandardAlpha

      private boolean useStandardAlpha(double temperature)

      Returns true when the standard PR alpha function should be used instead of the Mathias-Copeman form (supercritical temperatures or unset coefficients).

      Parameters:
      temperature - temperature in Kelvin
      Returns:
      true if the standard PR alpha should be used

    • allCoefficientsZero

      private boolean allCoefficientsZero()

      Checks whether all five Mathias-Copeman coefficients are effectively zero.

      Returns:
      true if every coefficient has an absolute value below 1e-20

    • polynomial

      private double[] polynomial(double temperature)

      Computes the inner Mathias-Copeman polynomial S = 1 + sum c_k (1 - sqrt(Tr))^k and its first two temperature derivatives.

      Parameters:
      temperature - temperature in Kelvin
      Returns:
      an array {S, dS/dT, d2S/dT2}
